zh.lifeSummary: 當Mary Rich於1526年出生時，她的父親Sir Richard Rich26歲，母親Elisabeth Jenkes23歲。她於1536年10月4日與Sir Thomas Wroth在英國米德萊可恩費爾德結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和7個女兒的父母。她去世於1591年10月17日在英國坎布利其爾易斯勒哈姆享年65歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭倫敦恩費爾德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Mary Rich于1526年出生时，她的父亲Sir Richard Rich26岁，母亲Elisabeth Jenkes23岁。她于1536年10月4日与Sir Thomas Wroth在英国米德萊可恩費爾德结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和7个女儿的父母。她去世于1591年10月17日在英国坎布利其爾易斯勒哈姆享年65岁，埋葬在英国英格兰倫敦恩費爾德。

nameMeanings.name: Rich
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname for a wealthy man (or perhaps in some cases an ironic nickname for a pauper), from Middle English, Old French riche ‘rich, wealthy’, a word of ancient Germanic origin, akin to ancient Germanic rīc ‘power(ful)’.
English: from the Middle English personal name Rich, a pet form of any of the post-Conquest names beginning in Rich-, such as Richer and especially Richard . Compare Rick .

English: either a topographic name from Middle English riche(Old English ric) ‘stream’, signifying one who lived beside a stream, as at Glynde Reach (Sussex), or perhaps a habitational name from the (now lost) village of Riche (Lincolnshire).

variants.name: Ritchie
meanings: Scottish: from a Scottish and northern English pet form of the personal name   Rich  , a short form of   Richard  . This surname is also common in Ulster.

variants.name: Ritch
meanings: English and Scottish (Orkney): either a variant of Rich , or perhaps from a short form of Ritchie , itself a diminutive based on Rich.

Altered form of German Ritsch, probably from a short form of an ancient Germanic personal name based on rīc ‘power(ful)’ or hrōd ‘fame, renown’; or an altered form of Swiss German Rütsch, Ruetsch, from Alemannic short forms of Rudolf .

variants.name: Riches
meanings: English (Norfolk and Suffolk): variant of Rich , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Richer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Normand, Armand, Fernand, Herve, Laurent, Marcel, Aurele, Donat, Elphege, Emile, Flore, Gilles.

English (East Anglia), French, and German: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements rīc ‘power(ful)’ + hari, heri ‘army’. The name was introduced into England by the Normans in the form Rich(i)er, Ricier, but was largely absorbed by the much more common Richard .

Altered form of French Héricher: from the ancient Germanic personal name Harigari, composed of the elements hari ‘army’ and gari (from garwa) ‘ready, eager’ or gari ‘spear’, or a variant of Hérissé, a nickname for someone with spiky hair, from the adjective hérissé, a derivative of the verb se hérisser ‘to stand on end’.

variants.name: Riche
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Pierre, Ghislaine, Philippe, Yanick, Yvon.
French: nickname for a rich man or perhaps an ironical name for a pauper, from Old French riche ‘rich’. Compare Leriche .

variants.name: Richins
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Ricun, a diminutive of Rich , itself a pet form of Richard , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s. It appears that a dialectally different (Norman or Picard) form of the French name, with medial /-k-/, also survived in England for a time alongside forms with original /-tʃ-/.

nameMeanings.name: Mary
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: When Mary Rich was born in 1526, her father, Sir Richard Rich, was 26 and her mother, Elisabeth Jenkes, was 23. She married Sir Thomas Wroth on 4 October 1536, in Enfield, Middlesex, England. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 7 daughters. She died on 17 October 1591, in Isleham, Cambridgeshire, England, at the age of 65, and was buried in Enfield, London, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Mary Rich est née en 1526, son père, Sir Richard Rich, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Elisabeth Jenkes, avait 23 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Thomas Wroth le 4 octobre 1536, à Enfield, Middlesex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 7 filles. Elle est décédée le 17 octobre 1591, à Isleham, Cambridgeshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 65 ans, et a été enterrée à Enfield, Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Mary Rich 1526 geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Richard Rich 26 und ihre Mutter Elisabeth Jenkes 23. Sie hat am 4. Oktober 1536 in Enfield, Middlesex, England Sir Thomas Wroth geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne und 7 Töchter. Sie starb am 17. Oktober 1591 im Alter von 65 Jahren in Isleham, Cambridgeshire, England und wurde in Enfield, London, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Mary Rich è nata il nel 1526, sua padre, Sir Richard Rich, aveva 26 anni e sua madre, Elisabeth Jenkes, aveva 23 anni. Lei sposato Sir Thomas Wroth il 4 ottobre 1536, nel Enfield, Middlesex, England. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli e 7 figlie. Morì il 17 ottobre 1591, nel Isleham, Cambridgeshire, England, all'età di 65 anni, e fu sepolta a Enfield, London, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Mary Richが1526年に生まれた時，父Sir Richard Richは26歳，母Elisabeth Jenkesは23歳であった。1536年10月4日にイギリスミッドレセックスエンフィールドでSir Thomas Wrothと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と7人の娘の親であった。彼女は1591年10月17日にイギリスカンブリジェシュアイアイスレハムで65歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスロンドンエンフィールドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Mary Rich(이)가 1526년에 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Richard Rich(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elisabeth Jenkes(은)는 23세였다. 그녀는 1536년 10월 4일 영국 미들레세스 필드에서 Sir Thomas Wroth(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1591년 10월 17일 영국 캄브리지실르 이슬레함에서 65세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 런던 필드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Mary Rich nasceu em 1526, seu pai, Sir Richard Rich, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Elisabeth Jenkes, tinha 23 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Thomas Wroth em 4 de outubro de 1536, em Enfield, Middlesex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 7 filhas. Ela faleceu em 17 de outubro de 1591, em Isleham, Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra, com 65 anos, e foi sepultada em Enfield, London, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Mary Rich родилась в 1526 г., её отцу по имени Sir Richard Rich было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Elisabeth Jenkes, 23. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Thomas Wroth 4 октября 1536 г. в местности энфиелд, миддлесекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 7 дочерей. Она умерла 17 октября 1591 г. в местности ислэам, камбриджешир, Англия в возрасте 65 лет и была похоронена в местности энфиелд, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Mary Rich nació en 1526, su papá, Sir Richard Rich, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Elisabeth Jenkes, tenía 23 años. Se casó con Sir Thomas Wroth el 4 de octubre de 1536, en Enfield, Middlesex, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 7 hijas. Murió el 17 de octubre de 1591, en Isleham, Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 65 años, y fue sepultada en Enfield, London, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
